title: Associations between gestational exposure to perfluoroalkyl substances, fetal growth, and the mediation effect of thyroid hormones.
ehr_evidence: The study used data from the Ezhou birth cohort study, which is a primary cohort study.
summary: The study investigates the associations between maternal serum PFAS concentrations and neonatal growth metrics, specifically examining the mediating role of thyroid hormones (FT3 and FT4).
key_findings: PFNA and PFHxS were negatively associated with birth length.
PFOA and PFHxS were negatively associated with birth head circumference.
FT3 mediated 36.7% of the negative association between PFNA and birth length.
FT4 mediated 30.8% of the effect of PFOA on head circumference.
population: 374 mother-infant pairs from the Ezhou birth cohort study (2019-2020)
data_availability_statement: The datasets used and/or analysed during the current study available from the corresponding author on reasonable request.
